name: WebSocketFramework.Server

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment:
The messages processed by our `update` function.

`Connection`, `Disconnection`, and `SocketMessage` from through the `inputPort` from the Node.JS code. `FirstTick` and `Tick` are used to track time. `Noop` does nothing.

unions.name: WrappedModel
unions.comment: A type wrapper to prevent recursive types in `Model`.

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment:
The server application model.

`program` creates one of these, via a call to `init`.
aliases.type: { servermodel : servermodel, userFunctions : UserFunctions servermodel message gamestate player, state : ServerState gamestate player, gameidDict : Dict Socket GameId, playeridDict : Dict GameId (List PlayerId), socketsDict : Dict GameId (List Socket), deathWatch : List DeathWatch, deathWatchGameids : DeathWatchGameids, time : Time, seed : Seed }

aliases.name: ServerMessageSender
aliases.comment:
User function that is called to send the response(s) to a request.

This will usually call `sentToOne` and/or `sendToMany` with the `message` emitted by the `ServiceMessageProcessor` in the `UserFunctions` passed to `program`.

The first `message` is the request that came from client to server. The second `message` is the response. If no response is returned by the `ServiceMessageProcessor`, this function is not called.
aliases.type: WrappedModel servermodel message gamestate player -> Socket -> ServerState gamestate player -> message -> message -> ( WrappedModel servermodel message gamestate player, Cmd Msg )

aliases.name: UserFunctions
aliases.comment:
User functions that get called by the generic server code.

`encodeDecode` is used to translate messages to and from strings.

`messageProcessor` processes a client request into state changes and a response message.

`messageSender` decides what to do with the response message.

`messageToGameid` extracts a GameId from a message, if there is one.

`inputPort` and `outputPort` are the ports used to communicate with the Node.js code.
aliases.type: { encodeDecode : EncodeDecode message, messageProcessor : ServerMessageProcessor gamestate player message, messageSender : ServerMessageSender servermodel message gamestate player, messageToGameid : Maybe (MessageToGameid message), inputPort : InputPort Msg, outputPort : OutputPort Msg }

values.name: init
values.comment:
Return the initial `Model` and a `Cmd` to get the current time.

Usually called for you by `program`.
values.type: servermodel -> UserFunctions servermodel message gamestate player -> Maybe gamestate -> ( Model servermodel message gamestate player, Cmd Msg )

values.name: newGameid
values.comment:
Generate a random GameId string, ensuring that it is not already assigned to a game.

Will not be used by servers that have no concept of a game.
values.type: WrappedModel servermodel message gamestate player -> ( GameId, WrappedModel servermodel message gamestate player )

values.name: newPlayerid
values.comment:
Generate a random PlayerId string, ensuring that it is not already assigned to a player.

Will not be used by servers that have no concept of a player.
values.type: WrappedModel servermodel message gamestate player -> ( PlayerId, WrappedModel servermodel message gamestate player )

values.name: program
values.comment:
Create the top-level application program.

You will usually use the result of this function as the value of `main` in your top-level module.

Most servers will not need to use the `servermodel`, but it's a place to stash extra server-wide state that doesn't make sense in the game-specific `gamestate`.
values.type: servermodel -> UserFunctions servermodel message gamestate player -> Maybe gamestate -> Program Never (Model servermodel message gamestate player) Msg

values.name: sendToMany
values.comment: Send a message to a multiple sockets.
values.type: MessageEncoder message -> message -> OutputPort Msg -> List Socket -> Cmd Msg

values.name: sendToOne
values.comment: Send a message to a single socket.
values.type: MessageEncoder message -> message -> OutputPort Msg -> Socket -> Cmd Msg
